title: torilis arvensis subsp. purpurea
intro: Torilis arvensis subsp. purpurea, also known as purple hedge-parsley, is a plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of purple hedge-parsley include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Purple hedge-parsley can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
